title: How President Trump's Tariffs Changed World Trade
summary: One year ago, President Donald Trump started a trade war by putting taxes called tariffs on goods from other countries like China. He wanted to help American factories and make money for the government. Now, tariffs in the US are much higher, averaging about 10% compared to 2.5% before. This has changed global trade in four main ways. First, China fought back with its own tariffs, causing trade between the US and China to drop a lot. US imports from China fell by 30%, and exports to China fell by over 25%. Second, Chinese goods now make up less than 10% of US imports, down from over 20% in 2016. Third, the US is buying more from Vietnam and Mexico as Chinese companies invest there. Fourth, experts say this shift is likely to last, meaning trade won't go back to how it was before. Overall, Trump's tariffs have made trade between the US and China much smaller and changed where the US gets its goods from.

keywords.explanation: taxes that countries put on goods from other countries
when countries fight by raising taxes on each other's goods
goods that a country buys from other countries
goods that a country sells to other countries
when two countries trade less with each other

questions.question: What did President Trump start one year ago?
What are tariffs?
options: Taxes on goods from other countries

questions.question: How much did US imports from China drop last year?
Which countries did the US buy more from instead of China?
What percentage of US imports were Chinese goods at the end of last year?
Do experts think trade will go back to how it was before?
options: No, it will likely stay changed
Yes, it will go back soon

background_read: This article talks about how President Donald Trump's tariffs, which are taxes on goods from other countries, have changed world trade over one year. It explains that tariffs in the US went up, causing trade with China to drop a lot, and the US started buying more from other countries like Vietnam and Mexico. Experts say these changes might last, meaning trade won't be the same as before. This helps us understand how countries can affect each other's economies through taxes on goods.
Article_Structure: WHO: President Donald Trump and countries like China, Vietnam, and Mexico
WHAT: Tariffs (taxes on goods) were increased, leading to less trade between the US and China and more trade with other countries
WHERE: In the US and globally, affecting trade between countries
WHY: To help American factories, make money for the government, and change how the US trades with other countries

perspectives.description: The tariffs caused big drops in trade between the US and China, showing how taxes can quickly change global business.
